channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Dodgers' Mookie Betts still struggling with illness, status for opening day in doubt
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers shortstop Mookie Betts is still struggling to recover from an illness, with manager Dave Roberts pessimistic that the eight-time All-Star will be ready for opening day against the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday in Japan.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about March Madness if you don’t follow college basketball
item.description: It's the basketball world's term to reference the upsets, chaos, remarkable plays and historic moments that are sure to happen in postseason tournaments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bickerstaff blasts officials after Pistons receive 5 technical fouls in loss to Thunder
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff blasted the officials after Detroit was called for five technical fouls in the third quarter of a 113-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dick Vitale tears up on ACC Championship broadcast while talking about recent cancer battle
item.description: Dick Vitale became emotional Saturday night while talking about his most recent cancer battle while announcing top-ranked Duke's 73-62 victory over No. 13 Louisville in the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament championship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 6 St. John's beats Creighton 82-66 for 1st Big East Tournament crown in 25 years
item.description: RJ Luis Jr. scored all but two of his 29 points after halftime and No. 6 St. John’s made 14 straight shots from the field in the second half to beat Creighton 82-66 for its first Big East Tournament title in 25 years.
item.dc:creator: Mike Fitzpatrick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pirates announce ace Paul Skenes will start on opening day at Miami
item.description: BRADENTON, Fla. (AP) — Paul Skenes is going to start for the Pittsburgh Pirates on opening day. Pretty easy call for manager Derek Shelton. Skenes is coming off a dominant performance in his first season in the majors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plucky Lakers nearly beat Nuggets despite Luka Doncic, LeBron James among LA's sidelined starters
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Luka Doncic sat out the Los Angeles Lakers' 131-126 loss at Denver on Friday night, cheering on his scrappy, short-handed teammates who nearly pulled off a shocker without four starters.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poway and USD alum Connor Joe to play for his hometown Padres
item.description: As the San Diego Padres are gearing up for the start of the 2025 MLB season, a player who has his sights set on making the Opening Day roster with his hometown Friars is Poway resident Connor Joe.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: 3 more Norwegian ski jumpers suspended in cheating scandal
item.description: A video leaked to the International Ski and Snowboard Federation shows the illegal alterations being made.
item.dc:creator: Laura Meehan, NewsNation

item.title: A loaded Thursday: Highlights from SIX games are here
item.description: It was a humongous Thursday of action on the Western Slope as winter sports reach their crescendo and spring sports continue to roll.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: The Grand Valley's Muay Thai team showed they are not to be messed with
item.description: Out of 36 competing teams from all across the Mountain West, the boys from the Valley finished third.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Boys Lacrosse: La Jolla Country Day 17, Rancho Bernardo 4
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)-Boys Lacrosse in the north county on Tuesday evening with La Jolla Country Day beating Rancho Bernardo 17-4.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Jets and QB Justin Fields agree to a 2-year, $40 million deal, AP source says
item.description: Justin Fields was passed over by the New York Jets four years ago in the NFL draft. Now he's expected to be their No. 1 quarterback. Fields and the Jets agreed Monday to a two-year contract worth $40 million, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Report: Bears sign center Drew Dalman to 3-year deal
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have signed top free agent center Drew Dalman, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter. The 26-year-old former Falcon completes an overhaul of the Bears' interior offensive line after Poles and company jumped the market by trading for guards Joe Thuney and Jonah Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Nearing the end of a short spring, Cubs and Dodgers have made final preparations for season openers in Japan
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Shohei Ohtani's bat is ready for the defending World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers, while Shota Imanaga has looked good on the mound for the Chicago Cubs with less than a week remaining before baseball's regular-season opener in Japan.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Soldier Field to host Premier League doubleheader this summer
item.description: The Premier League Summer Series is coming to Chicago this summer, featuring four Premier League clubs - AFC Bournemouth, Everton, Manchester United, and West Ham United - in a doubleheader at Soldier Field on Wednesday, July 30.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history at $40 million per year
item.description: Myles Garrett is staying in Cleveland after the Browns gave him a record four-year contract extension that makes the four-time All-Pro edge rusher the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9
